title: Strengthening Regulatory Oversight on Street Food Vendors to Prevent Salmonella
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Street     food     plays     a   vital   role   in urban   food     culture  ,   offering     affordable     and     accessible     meals     to     millions  .   However  ,   inadequate     hygiene     practices     and     weak     regulatory     oversight     have     made     street     foods     a     significant     source     of     Salmonella  -related     foodborne     illnesses  .   Strengthening     regulatory     frameworks     can     minimize     risks     by     enforcing     hygiene     standards  ,   regular     inspections  ,   and     food     safety     training     for     vendors  . Investment in   portable   testing   technology  ,   community-based     surveillance  ,   and     public     awareness     campaigns     can     further     enhance     prevention  .   Coordinated     efforts     between     local     authorities  ,   health     departments  ,   and     food   vendor   associations   are   essential     to     create     a     safer     street     food     environment  .   Effective     regulatory     oversight     will     protect     public     health  ,   boost     consumer     confidence  ,   and     support     the   informal   economy     by     encouraging     safe     and     sustainable     food     vending     practices  .
